content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

WALTER L. NOSSAMAN
(1885-1964)

LOS ANGELES
THIRTY-FIRST FLOOR
555 SOUTH FLOWER STREET
LOS ANGELES, CA 90071-1602
(213) 618-7800

IRVINE
SUITE 1800
LAKESHORE TOWERS
18101 VON KARMAN AVENUE
IRVINE, CA 92714-1007
(714) 553-7800

SUITE 1000
915 L STREET
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814-3701
TELEPHONE (916) 442-8888
FACSIMILE (916) 442-0382

WARREN ELLIOTT
OF COUNSEL

SAN FRANCISCO
THIRTY-FOURTH FLOOR
50 CALIFORNIA STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94111-4712
(415) 398-3600

WASHINGTON, D.C.
SUITE 800
1227 25TH STREET, N.W.
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20037-1156
(202) 293-0100

August 12, 1992

REFER TO FILE NUMBER

Hand-Delivered

Mr. Wayne Ordos
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California  95814

     RE:  Operation Gobble: Charitable Event By Members
          of the California Water Association ("CWA")

Dear Mr. Ordos:

     Thank you for meeting with me in your offices on Friday,
August 7, 1992, to discuss the 1992 ground rules for Operation
Gobble, a program which has been described in previous
correspondence. As we discussed, the California Water
Association ("CWA") is once again poised to distribute
thousands of holiday turkeys to needy families throughout
California, assuming that we can agree upon reasonable
limitations which will permit the charitable gifts to be
recognized as such for federal and state tax purposes. As we
have also discussed, to the extent that a contribution is
deemed by tax authorities be a political contribution, it is
likely to be regarded as having been made with a political
rather than a donative intent. In any event, CWA is obviously
not engaged in anything other than a charitable endeavor and
can do much more with the incentive and aid of tax laws
recognizing charitable contributions.

     At our meeting, you expressed a great deal of sympathy for
the program. We both agreed that the five "conditions" agreed
to between the FPPC and CWA in 1991 remain viable and should be
re-adopted as conditions for the 1992 program to avoid the
making of inadvertent political contributions to legislators
who may support the program.

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT


Mr. Wayne Ordos
August 12, 1992
RE: Operation Gobble
Page 2


     In my July 2, 1992, correspondence with Mr. Davidian, I
raised the additional problem of comments which were added to
the 1991 conditions in a letter from Mr. Davidian to me. (See
Adkisson Advice Letter (N. A-91-512; Nov. 18, 1992.) Mr.
Davidian had commented that the conditions might or might not
be sufficient for the 1992 season and suggested that "it would
be better" if CWA did not rely on legislators to help designate
worthy charities or consult with legislators about scheduled
events at which the holiday turkeys are to be given away.

     As I emphasized in my July 2, 1992, letter, it would be
inappropriate to bar a legislator from designating a worthy
charity or from being consulted about an event, unless the
circumstances resulted in either a representation that the
source of the donation was the elected official, or in an
exclusive forum for the elected official aimed at aiding in
election efforts.

     Since these events are scheduled for after the election
cycle, and the original five conditions already prohibit an
exclusive forum or the crediting of a donation to an elected
official, any additional bar to free communication with an
elected official is unnecessary to avoid a political
contribution. Since the additional conditions are unnecessary,
they would, in my view, constitute overreaching by the
Commission and would unnecessarily interfere with CWA's and its
members' rights of speech and association.

     Of equal importance, as we discussed, the additional
conditions may, as a practical matter, jeopardize the program,
given the history of involvement of legislators and the risk
that such broad conditions would result in every legislator
reporting the turkeys as political contributions even in
circumstances where such reporting is unnecessary. In short,
we strongly urge you to permit the 1992 program to go forward
without adding additional barriers such as those eluded to in
the Chairman's 1991, advice letter.

     During our discussion we also agreed that two additional
safeguards might serve to better protect the integrity of the
charitable program. First, you requested an outline of the
program questionnaire packet which will be utilized to monitor
and administer Operation Gobble in 1992. I have attached a

JDA/jw 756

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT

Mr. Wayne Ordos
August 12, 1992
RE: Operation Gobble
Page 3

rough outline of the packet to this correspondence. The
purpose of the packet is to maintain uniformity in the program
throughout California so that we may identify any rare
instances in which the conditions have not been met. In such
instances, we intend to recommend to legislators that they
report the value of the turkeys as in-kind political
contributions. Hopefully, however, through uniformity, the
charitable intent of each gift can be maintained by strict
adherence to the conditions.

The second safeguard which you suggested at our meeting is
also acceptable. You requested that a listing of the times and
locations of the events be provided in advance of the events,
presumably to facilitate discretionary observance by FPPC
officials or members of the general public. I am informed by
leaders within my client's organization that this should pose
no problem.

I have had a difficult time, however, with a third request
you made at our meeting. Essentially, you requested that I
compile a summary of the advice I gave to my client regarding
specific events and legislators during the 1991 season. Upon
further reflection I must decline because of the waiver of
attorney-client privilege involved in such a summary. I have
not been able to devise a way to share these details without
inadvertently compromising my client's right to private counsel
and my firm's integrity in its work product. I would be more
than happy to discuss the general nature of my oversight for
1991 and 1992. However, I have concluded that specific
reporting of my advice and communications would serve no
purpose.

I would appreciate a response by no later than the end of
August, since my client plans to convene a meeting to authorize
and plan the 1992 program in early September.

Sincerely,

John D. Adkisson of
NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT

JDA/jw 756

sections.question_synthetic: The letter addresses conditions and safeguards for Operation Gobble, a charitable turkey distribution program by the California Water Association, and discusses whether additional restrictions proposed by the FPPC are necessary to avoid characterizing the gifts as political contributions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming letter from counsel to FPPC, not an FPPC advice letter response. No formal question or conclusion sections present. Document references an earlier FPPC Advice Letter (A-91-512) but this letter itself is advocacy correspondence from private counsel rather than FPPC guidance.

embedding.summary: This is correspondence from counsel to the California Water Association to FPPC staff regarding Operation Gobble, a charitable holiday turkey distribution program. The letter discusses previously agreed conditions, disputes additional proposed restrictions, and proposes two safeguards (a program questionnaire packet and advance event listing) while declining to provide a summary of prior legal advice due to attorney-client privilege.
